race.name: Le Loop
race.tagline: Ride the full Tour de France route—3,429km, 172,566ft of hellish glory—one week ahead of the pros.

vitals.field_size: 250–300 riders annually, capped for elite logistics
vitals.start_time: Morning group starts, 13-15hr cutoffs per stage
vitals.registration: £8,950–£9,950 covers pro-level support, hotels, meals, bike transport, jersey; requires £2,500 charity fundraising
vitals.prize_purse: None—non-competitive charity epic, no podiums or cash
vitals.aid_stations: Moto feeds, team cars, helicopters, broom wagon like the pros
vitals.cutoff_time: 13-15 hours per stage to match TdF rhythm
distance_options: Full Le Loop: 2129mi, 172566ft — exact TdF route, 21 stages
Pyrenees Week: 435mi, ~80000ft — brutal high mountains incl. Tourmalet
Alps Week: 404mi, ~75000ft — Alpe d'Huez, Col de la Loze crushers

climate.primary: Variable French summer—mild north to scorching south
climate.description: North starts mild at 15–25°C with 50% rain risk; south/coast hits 25–35°C heatwaves; mountains cool to 10–25°C with storms or snow above 2,000m. Sunny 70% but TdF history packs 20–30% rain stages—pack for 0–40°C extremes.

terrain.primary: TdF masterpiece: flats, hills, Pyrenees/Alps crushers
terrain.surface: 100% pristine TdF asphalt—smooth, swoopy descents begging for speed. No gravel, pure road paradise from coastal rollers to hairpin hell. Police-closed for empty-road bliss.

gravel_god_rating.tier_note: Prestige 5 catapults this TdF replica into T1 elite—ultimate amateur bucket-list badge of honor. Pro-level support on closed roads screams value despite price tag. Only field depth keeps it from perfection.

logistics.explanation: Pro-team treatment: mechanics, physios, motos, heli-feeds, bus transfers—flawless. They ship your bike, book 4* hotels, handle every detail. Bucket-list logistics that make Hincapie retreats look amateur.

length.explanation: 3,429km over 21 stages? That's TdF immortality for mortals. No half-measures—full epic or bust. Dwarfs every gran fondo pretender.

technicality.explanation: Paved perfection but those Alps hairpins and Ventoux winds demand respect. Descents can wreck you if cocky. Solid road test, not gravel chaos.

elevation.explanation: 172,566ft matches TdF brutality—Tourmalet, Alpe d'Huez, Loze. Daily 5,000m+ stages forge legends. Vertical that breaks souls and builds heroes.

climate.explanation: French summer roulette: heat hell south, rain north, mountain roulette. 70% sun but TdF rain legacy haunts. Train versatile or suffer.

altitude.explanation: Pyrenees/Alps giants over 2,000m—rarefied air that punches harder than pros. Snow-risk summits in July? Epic badge-of-honor territory.

adventure.explanation: Closed TdF roads, empty cols, police escorts—one week ahead of Pogacar circus. Cross-France odyssey through villages worshipping cycling gods.

prestige.explanation: Brag-rights zenith: 'I rode the full TdF route.' Charity halo plus pro-path timing = instant legend status. Outshines every sportive sticker.

race_quality.explanation: Non-competitive but pro-pure: exact route, full support, no racing egos. Organizers nail TdF replica better than any copycat.

experience.explanation: Camaraderie in suffering, decorated villages, post-climb beers—pure joy. 'Tour for amateurs' delivers feels money can't buy.

community.explanation: Club cyclists, charity warriors, elites bonding over pain. English-speaking vibe strong but capped field limits depth. Tight-knit sufferfest.

field_depth.explanation: 250-300 cap keeps logistics tight but dilutes pro-amateur mix. No peloton swarm—just committed mortals. Quality over quantity.

value.explanation: £10k buys TdF dream—support, hotels, glory. Fundraising doubles impact. Steep but unmatched ROI in memories.

expenses.explanation: £9k+ entry stings, plus flights/insurance. High-income playground. Worth it for the worthy, wallet-killer for rest.

biased_opinion.summary: Le Loop isn't a ride; it's stealing the Tour de France from under the pros' noses. 3,429km of exact TdF asphalt, closed roads, pro support—one week early for empty glory. Charity soul elevates it beyond gran fondo cash-grabs. Brutal elevation and logistics forge unbreakable bonds. Pricey? Yes. Life-changing? Absolutely.
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Ultimate cycling pilgrimage for serious adventurers. Sell a kidney if needed—regrets only for no-shows. Prestige eclipses every alternative.

final_verdict.one_liner: Ride the Tour de France or forever wish you had.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you're fit(ish), funded, and crave immortality—train 10,000km prior. Skip if budgets tight or prefer podium scraps— this is completionist church.
final_verdict.alternatives: Hincapie Gran Fondo (hincapie), Haute Route Alps (haute-route-alps), Tour of the Alps (tour-of-alps)

logistics.airport: Fly Paris CDG/ORY or Lille LIL for northern starts; regional Nice/Toulouse for mountains. Le Loop buses from Paris if needed; ship bike boxes ahead.
logistics.lodging_strategy: All-inclusive 2-4* hotels mirroring TdF teams—booked seamlessly. Stage-to-stage transfers via luxury buses.
logistics.food: Pro feeds on moto/heli, hotel breakfast/dinners—fuel like champions. No foraging required.
logistics.packet_pickup: On-site at Grand Depart with jersey/kits; bike prep by mechanics.
logistics.parking: N/A—full bike transport, no personal vehicles needed.

history.origin_story: David Duffield rode the 2004 TdF route solo ahead of pros, sparking Le Loop. Launched 2006 as UK charity challenge. Evolved into polished epic riding exact TdF path one week early. Raised £3M+ for causes like Leukaemia Care.
notable_moments: 2014: First £1M+ charity haul on TdF centenary
history.reputation: Gold-standard charity TdF replica—flawless execution, life-changing vibes. Riders rave about support and serenity on pro roads.

course_description.character: 21 TdF stages averaging 163km: 40% flat sprints, 30% hilly, 30% mountain Armageddon. Rolling plains to Pyrenees crushers, Alps hairpins, Provence sun. Paved poetry through cycling-mad villages.

suffering_zones.label: Col du Tourmalet
suffering_zones.desc: Pyrenees behemoth at 6-7% endless grind to 2,115m. Iconic TdF col where legends crack. Le Loopers conquer empty.
suffering_zones.terrain_detail: Steep paved ramps, exposed high plateau

suffering_zones.label: Alpe d'Huez
suffering_zones.desc: 21 numbered hairpins, 1,186m gain at 8% average. Switchback hell under crowds (minus pros). Pure suffering summit.

suffering_zones.label: Mont Ventoux
suffering_zones.desc: Lunar 'Giant of Provence'—gravelly summit option, howling winds. TdF breaker since 1951. Le Loop preview sans chaos.

course_description.signature_challenge: 172,566ft total—up to 5,000m/stage in mountains. Time cutoffs force relentless pacing amid heat, crashes, transfers. True TdF soul-crusher.

guide_variables.race_weather: Variable: mild north (15-25C), hot south (25-35C), cool mountains (10-25C); rain/storms possible

non_negotiables.why: 21 stages demand unbreakable engine—flats build speed, mountains forge legs. Miss this, broom wagon awaits. TdF ain't forgiving.
Charity core—fundraise or no bib. Builds community, offsets costs. Slackers sidelined.
Ventoux/Tourmalet air thins; southern scorch tests limits. Prep or perish in extremes.

youtube_data.search_query: Le Loop gran fondo cycling Tour de France Route

videos.title: L’ETAPE LAS VEGAS gran fondo by tour of France

quotes.text: The climb is an average of 4% so not super steep and I will say kind of wind dependent. The wind today it's a strong wind, so pretty quickly I was doing 500 watts and hemorrhaging time.

The event will have a closed course through Red Rocks Canyon. When you think Vegas I know your first thought it's probably not bike riding but I'm here to show you that the bike riding is actually pretty awesome.

key_challenges.name: Red Rocks Canyon Climb
key_challenges.description: Average 4% grade climb that is highly wind dependent. Strong unfavorable winds can force riders to push 500 watts while still losing time on flat and downhill sections.

terrain_notes.text: The climb features a straight flat/downhill section where riders should be recovering, but headwinds can force high wattage efforts even on these easier gradients.

race_day_tips.text: Wind conditions are critical for performance - unfavorable headwinds can significantly impact times and require much higher power output throughout the climb.

additional_quotes.text: Le Loop generously offered to send someone out and help me film the more common coveted Strava segment closer to town. The event will have a closed course through Red Rocks Canyon.

rider_intel.search_text: Le Loop features a Vegas edition with a closed course through Red Rocks Canyon. The main climb averages 4% grade but is highly wind dependent, with strong headwinds forcing riders to maintain 500+ watts even on flat and downhill recovery sections. The scenic red rock terrain provides beautiful views but challenging conditions. One rider noted needing 429 watts for 13 minutes to set competitive times on the main climbing segment. The course offers a more accessible alternative to the epic Mount Charleston climb further from town. Wind conditions appear to be the primary factor affecting performance and race times.
